content:
# Exodus Chapter 28 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios ~48v–50r (PDF pp. 96–99); Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 70–71 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_028_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_028_page_002.jpg`, `EXO_028_page_003.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_028_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_028_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — THE PRIESTLY VESTMENTS:** The Leningrad Codex presents Exodus 28 across three folio pages in standard prose column format with full Masoretic vocalization and cantillation. This chapter shifts decisively from sacred architecture to sacred personnel — from the tabernacle and its furnishings to the priests who will serve within it. The chapter opens by setting apart Aaron and his sons (vv. 1–5), then details the priestly garments in descending order of complexity: the ephod (vv. 6–14), the breastpiece of judgment with its twelve stones and the Urim and Thummim (vv. 15–30), the robe of the ephod (vv. 31–35), the gold plate inscribed "Holy to YHWH" (vv. 36–38), the woven tunic, turban, and sash (v. 39), garments for Aaron's sons (vv. 40–41), and the linen undergarments (vv. 42–43). The Vaticanus manuscript (pp. 70–71) presents the LXX text but with a dramatically different arrangement: the LXX reorders the content extensively, particularly in the breastpiece section, and adds material not found in the MT.

---

## The Appointment of the Priests (vv. 1–5)

**1** And you [ve-attah], bring near [haqrev] to you [eilekha] Aaron [et-Aharon] your brother [akhikha], and his sons [ve-et-banav] with him [itto], from the midst [mi-tokh] of the sons of Israel [benei Yisrael], to be a priest [le-khahano] to me [li]: Aaron [Aharon], Nadab [Nadav] and Abihu [va-Avihu], Eleazar [El'azar] and Ithamar [ve-Itamar], the sons of [benei] Aaron [Aharon].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאַתָּה הַקְרֵב אֵלֶיךָ אֶת־אַהֲרֹן אָחִיךָ וְאֶת־בָּנָיו אִתּוֹ מִתּוֹךְ בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל לְכַהֲנוֹ־לִי אַהֲרֹן נָדָב וַאֲבִיהוּא אֶלְעָזָר וְאִיתָמָר בְּנֵי אַהֲרֹן. **Key terms:** הַקְרֵב (haqrev) = hiphil imperative of קרב (qarav, "to draw near") — "cause to draw near"; the priestly role is defined spatially — priests are those drawn near to YHWH. לְכַהֲנוֹ (le-khahano) = piel infinitive construct of כהן (kohen) + 3ms suffix — "to serve as priest to me"; the root כהן may relate to an original sense of "to stand, attend." LXX: καὶ σὺ προσαγάγου πρὸς σεαυτὸν τόν τε Ααρων τὸν ἀδελφόν σου... — προσάγω ("bring near") for haqrev.

**2** And you shall make [ve-asita] garments [vigdei] of holiness [qodesh] for Aaron [le-Aharon] your brother [akhikha], for glory [le-khavod] and for beauty [u-le-tif'aret].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ בִגְדֵי־קֹדֶשׁ לְאַהֲרֹן אָחִיךָ לְכָבוֹד וּלְתִפְאָרֶת. **Key terms:** בִגְדֵי־קֹדֶשׁ (vigdei-qodesh) = "garments of holiness" — not merely "holy garments" but garments whose essence is holiness; from בגד (beged, "garment, covering"). לְכָבוֹד וּלְתִפְאָרֶת (le-khavod u-le-tif'aret) = "for glory and for beauty" — kavod = "weight, glory, honor"; tif'eret = "beauty, splendor, adornment" (from פאר, pa'ar, "to glorify"). LXX: ποιήσεις στολὴν ἁγίαν Ααρων... εἰς τιμὴν καὶ δόξαν — τιμή ("honor") and δόξα ("glory") for kavod and tif'eret.

**3** And you [ve-attah] shall speak [tedabber] to [el] all [kol] the wise [khakhmei] of heart [lev] whom [asher] I have filled [milletiv] with a spirit [ruakh] of wisdom [khokhmah], and they shall make [ve-asu] the garments of [et-bigdei] Aaron [Aharon] to consecrate him [le-qadesho], to be a priest [le-khahano] to me [li].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאַתָּה תְּדַבֵּר אֶל־כָּל־חַכְמֵי־לֵב אֲשֶׁר מִלֵּאתִיו רוּחַ חָכְמָה וְעָשׂוּ אֶת־בִּגְדֵי אַהֲרֹן לְקַדְּשׁוֹ לְכַהֲנוֹ־לִי. **Key terms:** חַכְמֵי־לֵב (khakhmei-lev) = "wise of heart" — wisdom in Hebrew is localized in the heart (lev), not the mind; here it denotes skilled artisanship. מִלֵּאתִיו (milletiv) = piel perfect 1cs of מלא (male, "to fill") + 3ms suffix — "I have filled him"; God is the source of the artisan's skill. LXX: σὺ δὲ λάλησον πᾶσι τοῖς σοφοῖς τῇ διανοίᾳ — σοφός τῇ διανοίᾳ ("wise in understanding") for khakhmei-lev.

**4** And these [ve-elleh] are the garments [ha-begadim] which [asher] they shall make [ya'asu]: a breastpiece [khoshen] and an ephod [ve-efod] and a robe [u-me'il] and a tunic [u-khetonet] of checkered work [tashbets] and a turban [mitsnefet] and a sash [ve-avnet]; and they shall make [ve-asu] garments [vigdei] of holiness [qodesh] for Aaron [le-Aharon] your brother [akhikha] and for his sons [u-le-vanav] to be a priest [le-khahano] to me [li].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֵלֶּה הַבְּגָדִים אֲשֶׁר יַעֲשׂוּ חֹשֶׁן וְאֵפוֹד וּמְעִיל וּכְתֹנֶת תַּשְׁבֵּץ מִצְנֶפֶת וְאַבְנֵט. **Key terms:** חֹשֶׁן (khoshen) = "breastpiece" — etymology uncertain; possibly from a root meaning "beauty" or "protection." אֵפוֹד (efod) = "ephod" — a shoulder-garment; etymology debated. מְעִיל (me'il) = "robe" — from עלל (alal, "to go up"); an upper garment. תַּשְׁבֵּץ (tashbets) = "checkered work, woven settings" — from שׁבץ (shavats, "to weave in checker pattern"). מִצְנֶפֶת (mitsnefet) = "turban, headdress" — from צנף (tsanaf, "to wind, wrap"). אַבְנֵט (avnet) = "sash, girdle." LXX: the LXX lists the garments in a different order and adds τὸ περιστήθιον ("chest-piece") for khoshen.

**5** And they [ve-hem] shall take [yiqkhu] the gold [et-ha-zahav] and the blue [ve-et-ha-tekhelet] and the purple [ve-et-ha-argaman] and the scarlet yarn [ve-et-tola'at ha-shani] and the fine linen [ve-et-ha-shesh].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהֵם יִקְחוּ אֶת־הַזָּהָב וְאֶת־הַתְּכֵלֶת וְאֶת־הָאַרְגָּמָן וְאֶת־תּוֹלַעַת הַשָּׁנִי וְאֶת־הַשֵּׁשׁ. Five materials are listed — gold is added to the four textile materials used previously (26:1, 31, 36; 27:16), since gold thread is woven into the ephod and breastpiece.

---

## The Ephod (vv. 6–14)

**6** And they shall make [ve-asu] the ephod [et-ha-efod] of gold [zahav], of blue [tekhelet] and of purple [ve-argaman], of scarlet yarn [tola'at shani] and of fine twisted linen [ve-shesh moshzar], the work [ma'aseh] of a skilled craftsman [khoshev].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשׂוּ אֶת־הָאֵפֹד זָהָב תְּכֵלֶת וְאַרְגָּמָן תּוֹלַעַת שָׁנִי וְשֵׁשׁ מָשְׁזָר מַעֲשֵׂה חֹשֵׁב. **Key terms:** מַעֲשֵׂה חֹשֵׁב (ma'aseh khoshev) = "work of a skilled craftsman/designer" — from חשׁב (khashav, "to think, plan, devise"); the highest grade of textile work, used also for the parokhet (26:31). LXX: ποιήσουσιν τὴν ἐπωμίδα — ἐπωμίς ("shoulder-piece") for efod.

**7** Two [shtei] shoulder-pieces [khtfot] joining [khoverot] it shall have [yihyeh-lo] upon [el] its two [shnei] ends [qetsotav], and it shall be joined [ve-khubar].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads שְׁתֵּי כְתֵפֹת חֹבְרֹת יִהְיֶה־לּוֹ אֶל־שְׁנֵי קְצוֹתָיו וְחֻבָּר. **Key terms:** כְתֵפֹת (khtfot) = "shoulder-pieces" — from כתף (katef, "shoulder"); the ephod has two straps that go over the shoulders. חֹבְרֹת (khoverot) = qal active participle feminine plural of חבר (khavar, "to join") — "joining, attached." וְחֻבָּר (ve-khubar) = pual perfect — "and it shall be joined"; the shoulder-pieces are integral to the ephod.

**8** And the band of [ve-kheshev] his ephod [afudato] which [asher] is upon it [alav], according to its workmanship [ke-ma'asehu], from it [mimmenu] it shall be [yihyeh]: gold [zahav], blue [tekhelet] and purple [ve-argaman] and scarlet yarn [ve-tola'at shani] and fine twisted linen [ve-shesh moshzar].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְחֵשֶׁב אֲפֻדָּתוֹ אֲשֶׁר עָלָיו כְּמַעֲשֵׂהוּ מִמֶּנּוּ יִהְיֶה זָהָב תְּכֵלֶת וְאַרְגָּמָן וְתוֹלַעַת שָׁנִי וְשֵׁשׁ מָשְׁזָר. **Key terms:** חֵשֶׁב (kheshev) = "band, skilfully woven band" — related to חשׁב (khashav, "to devise"); the waistband of the ephod. אֲפֻדָּתוֹ (afudato) = "his ephod-work" — the girding mechanism. מִמֶּנּוּ (mimmenu) = "from it" — the band is one piece with the ephod, not a separate attachment.

**9** And you shall take [ve-laqakhta] two [shtei] stones [avnei] of onyx [shoham], and you shall engrave [u-fattakhta] upon them [aleihem] the names [shemot] of the sons of Israel [benei Yisrael].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְלָקַחְתָּ שְׁתֵּי אַבְנֵי־שֹׁהַם וּפִתַּחְתָּ עֲלֵיהֶם שְׁמוֹת בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל. **Key terms:** שֹׁהַם (shoham) = "onyx" (or possibly "lapis lazuli, carnelian") — the exact identification is debated. פִּתַּחְתָּ (fattakhta) = piel perfect of פתח (fatakh) — "you shall engrave, open"; engraving as "opening" the surface of the stone.

**10** Six [shishah] of their names [mi-shemotam] on [al] the one [ha-even ha-ekhat] stone, and the names [ve-et-shemot] of the six [ha-shishah] remaining [ha-notarim] on [al] the second [ha-even ha-shenit] stone, according to their birth-order [ke-toledotam].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads שִׁשָּׁה מִשְּׁמֹתָם עַל הָאֶבֶן הָאֶחָת וְאֶת־שְׁמוֹת הַשִּׁשָּׁה הַנּוֹתָרִים עַל הָאֶבֶן הַשֵּׁנִית כְּתוֹלְדֹתָם. **Key terms:** כְּתוֹלְדֹתָם (ke-toledotam) = "according to their generations/birth-order" — from ילד (yalad, "to bear"); the names are arranged chronologically by birth.

**11** The work [ma'aseh] of a stone-engraver [kharash even], engravings [pittuhei] of a seal [khotam], you shall engrave [tefattakh] the two [et-shtei] stones [ha-avanim] with the names [al-shemot] of the sons of Israel [benei Yisrael]; surrounded [musabbot] by settings [mishbetsot] of gold [zahav] you shall make [ta'aseh] them [otam].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads מַעֲשֵׂה חָרַשׁ אֶבֶן פִּתּוּחֵי חֹתָם תְּפַתַּח אֶת־שְׁתֵּי הָאֲבָנִים עַל־שְׁמֹת בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל מֻסַבֹּת מִשְׁבְּצוֹת זָהָב תַּעֲשֶׂה אֹתָם. **Key terms:** חָרַשׁ אֶבֶן (kharash even) = "craftsman of stone, stone-engraver." פִּתּוּחֵי חֹתָם (pittuhei khotam) = "engravings of a seal" — the same engraving quality used for signet rings. מִשְׁבְּצוֹת (mishbetsot) = "settings, filigree" — from שׁבץ (shavats, "to weave, set").

**12** And you shall put [ve-samta] the two [et-shtei] stones [ha-avanim] upon [al] the shoulder-pieces [kitefot] of the ephod [ha-efod] — stones [avnei] of remembrance [zikkaron] for the sons of Israel [li-vnei Yisrael]; and Aaron [Aharon] shall bear [ve-nasa] their names [et-shemotam] before [lifnei] YHWH upon [al] his two [shtei] shoulders [khtefav] as a remembrance [le-zikkaron].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשַׂמְתָּ אֶת־שְׁתֵּי הָאֲבָנִים עַל כִּתְפֹת הָאֵפֹד אַבְנֵי זִכָּרֹן לִבְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל וְנָשָׂא אַהֲרֹן אֶת־שְׁמוֹתָם לִפְנֵי יְהוָה עַל־שְׁתֵּי כְתֵפָיו לְזִכָּרֹן. **Key terms:** זִכָּרֹן (zikkaron) = "remembrance, memorial" — from זכר (zakhar, "to remember"); the stones are a physical prompt for divine remembrance of Israel. The priest carries the nation's identity on his shoulders before YHWH.

**13** And you shall make [ve-asita] settings [mishbetsot] of gold [zahav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ מִשְׁבְּצֹת זָהָב. A brief verse — gold filigree settings for mounting the onyx stones.

**14** And two [u-shtei] chains [sharshrot] of pure gold [zahav tahor]; twisted-rope [migbalot] you shall make [ta'aseh] them [otam], the work [ma'aseh] of a rope [avot]; and you shall put [ve-natattah] the chains [et-sharshrot] of rope-work [ha-avotot] upon [al] the settings [ha-mishbetsot].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּשְׁתֵּי שַׁרְשְׁרֹת זָהָב טָהוֹר מִגְבָּלֹת תַּעֲשֶׂה אֹתָם מַעֲשֵׂה עֲבֹת וְנָתַתָּה אֶת־שַׁרְשְׁרֹת הָעֲבֹתֹת עַל־הַמִּשְׁבְּצֹת. **Key terms:** שַׁרְשְׁרֹת (sharshrot) = "chains" — from שׁרשׁר (sharsheret, "chain"); reduplicated root suggesting linked segments. מִגְבָּלֹת (migbalot) = "twisted-rope, braided cords" — from גבל (gaval, "to twist, border"). עֲבֹת (avot) = "rope, cord, thick twisted cord."

---

## The Breastpiece of Judgment (vv. 15–30)

**15** And you shall make [ve-asita] a breastpiece [khoshen] of judgment [mishpat], the work [ma'aseh] of a skilled craftsman [khoshev]; like the work [ke-ma'aseh] of the ephod [efod] you shall make it [ta'asenu]: of gold [zahav], of blue [tekhelet] and of purple [ve-argaman] and of scarlet yarn [ve-tola'at shani] and of fine twisted linen [ve-shesh moshzar] you shall make it [ta'aseh oto].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ חֹשֶׁן מִשְׁפָּט מַעֲשֵׂה חֹשֵׁב כְּמַעֲשֵׂה אֵפֹד תַּעֲשֶׂנּוּ זָהָב תְּכֵלֶת וְאַרְגָּמָן וְתוֹלַעַת שָׁנִי וְשֵׁשׁ מָשְׁזָר תַּעֲשֶׂה אֹתוֹ. **Key terms:** חֹשֶׁן מִשְׁפָּט (khoshen mishpat) = "breastpiece of judgment" — mishpat = "judgment, decision, verdict" (from שׁפט, shafat, "to judge"); this breastpiece will contain the Urim and Thummim (v. 30), instruments of divine decision-making.

**16** Square [ravua] it shall be [yihyeh], doubled [kaful]; a span [zeret] its length [orkho] and a span [ve-zeret] its width [rokhbo].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads רָבוּעַ יִהְיֶה כָּפוּל זֶרֶת אָרְכּוֹ וְזֶרֶת רָחְבּוֹ. **Key terms:** כָּפוּל (kaful) = "doubled, folded double" — from כפל (kafal, "to double, fold"); the breastpiece is folded to create a pouch (for the Urim and Thummim). זֶרֶת (zeret) = "span" — the distance from thumb-tip to little-finger tip with hand spread, approximately 9 inches (half a cubit).

**17** And you shall set in it [u-mileta vo] a setting [millu'at] of stone [even]: four [arba'ah] rows [turim] of stone [even]. A row [tur] of ruby [odem], topaz [pitdah], and emerald [u-vareket] — the first [ha-rishon] row [ha-tur].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּמִלֵּאתָ בוֹ מִלֻּאַת אֶבֶן אַרְבָּעָה טוּרִים אָבֶן טוּר אֹדֶם פִּטְדָה וּבָרֶקֶת הַטּוּר הָאֶחָד. **Key terms:** מִלֻּאַת (millu'at) = "setting, filling" — from מלא (male, "to fill"); stones "fill" the settings. אֹדֶם (odem) = "ruby" or "carnelian" — from אדם (adam, "to be red"); a red stone. פִּטְדָה (pitdah) = "topaz" or "chrysolite." בָרֶקֶת (vareket) = "emerald" or "beryl" — from ברק (baraq, "to flash, gleam"). The exact identification of all twelve stones remains debated.

**18** And the second [ve-ha-tur ha-sheni] row: turquoise [nofekh], sapphire [sappir], and diamond [ve-yahalom].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהַטּוּר הַשֵּׁנִי נֹפֶךְ סַפִּיר וְיָהֲלֹם. **Key terms:** נֹפֶךְ (nofekh) = "turquoise" or "garnet." סַפִּיר (sappir) = "sapphire" or "lapis lazuli." יָהֲלֹם (yahalom) = "diamond" or "jasper" — from הלם (halam, "to strike").

**19** And the third [ve-ha-tur ha-shelishi] row: jacinth [leshem], agate [shevo], and amethyst [ve-akhlamah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהַטּוּר הַשְּׁלִישִׁי לֶשֶׁם שְׁבוֹ וְאַחְלָמָה. **Key terms:** לֶשֶׁם (leshem) = "jacinth" or "ligure." שְׁבוֹ (shevo) = "agate." אַחְלָמָה (akhlamah) = "amethyst" — possibly from חלם (khalam, "to dream"); ancient tradition associated amethyst with dreams.

**20** And the fourth [ve-ha-tur ha-revi'i] row: beryl [tarshish], onyx [ve-shoham], and jasper [ve-yashfeh]. They shall be set [musabbim] in gold [zahav] in their settings [be-millu'otam].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהַטּוּר הָרְבִיעִי תַּרְשִׁישׁ וְשֹׁהַם וְיָשְׁפֵה מְשֻׁבָּצִים זָהָב יִהְיוּ בְּמִלּוּאֹתָם. **Key terms:** תַּרְשִׁישׁ (tarshish) = "beryl" or "chrysolite" — named for the region of Tarshish. יָשְׁפֵה (yashfeh) = "jasper." מְשֻׁבָּצִים (meshubatsim) = pual participle of שׁבץ — "set in filigree."

**21** And the stones [ve-ha-avanim] shall be [tihyeyna] according to the names [al-shemot] of the sons of Israel [benei Yisrael]: twelve [shteim esreh], according to their names [al-shemotam]; engravings [pittuhei] of a seal [khotam], each [ish] according to his name [al-shemo] they shall be [tihyeyna], for the twelve [li-shneim asar] tribes [shevet].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהָאֲבָנִים תִּהְיֶיןָ עַל־שְׁמֹת בְּנֵי־יִשְׂרָאֵל שְׁתֵּים עֶשְׂרֵה עַל־שְׁמֹתָם פִּתּוּחֵי חוֹתָם אִישׁ עַל־שְׁמוֹ תִּהְיֶיןָ לִשְׁנֵי עָשָׂר שָׁבֶט. **Key terms:** אִישׁ עַל־שְׁמוֹ (ish al-shemo) = "each man according to his name" — ish here is distributive, "each one." Each stone bears one tribal name, making the breastpiece a miniature embodiment of all Israel over the priest's heart.

**22** And you shall make [ve-asita] upon [al] the breastpiece [ha-khoshen] chains [sharshrot] of twisted-rope [gablut], the work [ma'aseh] of rope [avot], of pure gold [zahav tahor].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ עַל־הַחֹשֶׁן שַׁרְשֹׁת גַּבְלֻת מַעֲשֵׂה עֲבֹת זָהָב טָהוֹר. **Key terms:** גַּבְלֻת (gablut) = "twisted, braided" — from גבל (gaval, "to twist"); the same braided gold chains described in v. 14.

**23** And you shall make [ve-asita] upon [al] the breastpiece [ha-khoshen] two [shtei] rings [tabb'ot] of gold [zahav], and you shall put [ve-natattah] the two [et-shtei] rings [ha-tabb'ot] upon [al] the two [shnei] ends [qetsot] of the breastpiece [ha-khoshen].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ עַל־הַחֹשֶׁן שְׁתֵּי טַבְּעוֹת זָהָב וְנָתַתָּ אֶת־שְׁתֵּי הַטַּבָּעוֹת עַל־שְׁנֵי קְצוֹת הַחֹשֶׁן. Two rings at the upper corners of the breastpiece.

**24** And you shall put [ve-natattah] the two [shtei] ropes [avotot] of gold [ha-zahav] upon [al] the two [shtei] rings [ha-tabb'ot], unto [el] the ends [qetsot] of the breastpiece [ha-khoshen].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְנָתַתָּה שְׁתֵּי עֲבֹתֹת הַזָּהָב עַל־שְׁתֵּי הַטַּבָּעֹת אֶל־קְצוֹת הַחֹשֶׁן. The gold rope-chains connect the upper rings of the breastpiece to the ephod's shoulder-pieces.

**25** And the two [ve-et shtei] ends [qetsot] of the two [shtei] ropes [ha-avotot] you shall put [titten] upon [al] the two [shtei] settings [ha-mishbetsot], and you shall put [ve-natattah] them upon [al] the shoulder-pieces [kitefot] of the ephod [ha-efod], on the front of its face [el-mul panav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֵת שְׁתֵּי קְצוֹת שְׁתֵּי הָעֲבֹתֹת תִּתֵּן עַל־שְׁתֵּי הַמִּשְׁבְּצוֹת וְנָתַתָּה עַל־כִּתְפוֹת הָאֵפֹד אֶל־מוּל פָּנָיו. **Key terms:** אֶל־מוּל פָּנָיו (el-mul panav) = "to the front of its face" — the chains attach at the front of the shoulder-pieces.

**26** And you shall make [ve-asita] two [shtei] rings [tabb'ot] of gold [zahav], and you shall put [ve-samta] them [otam] upon [al] the two [shnei] ends [qetsot] of the breastpiece [ha-khoshen], upon [al] its edge [sefato] which [asher] is toward [el-ever] the ephod [ha-efod], inward [baytah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ שְׁתֵּי טַבְּעוֹת זָהָב וְשַׂמְתָּ אֹתָם עַל־שְׁנֵי קְצוֹת הַחֹשֶׁן עַל־שְׂפָתוֹ אֲשֶׁר אֶל־עֵבֶר הָאֵפֹד בָּיְתָה. **Key terms:** בָּיְתָה (baytah) = "inward, toward the house/inside" — the lower rings are on the inner edge of the breastpiece, facing the ephod.

**27** And you shall make [ve-asita] two [shtei] rings [tabb'ot] of gold [zahav], and you shall put [ve-natattah] them [otam] upon [al] the two [shtei] shoulder-pieces [kitefot] of the ephod [ha-efod], below [mi-lemattah], on the front of its face [mi-mul panav], close to [le-ummat] its joining [mekhbarto], above [mi-ma'al] the band [le-kheshev] of the ephod [ha-efod].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ שְׁתֵּי טַבְּעוֹת זָהָב וְנָתַתָּה אֹתָם עַל־שְׁתֵּי כִתְפוֹת הָאֵפוֹד מִלְּמַטָּה מִמּוּל פָּנָיו לְעֻמַּת מַחְבַּרְתּוֹ מִמַּעַל לְחֵשֶׁב הָאֵפוֹד. **Key terms:** מַחְבַּרְתּוֹ (mekhbarto) = "its joining, its seam" — from חבר (khavar, "to join"); the point where the shoulder-piece meets the ephod body.

**28** And they shall bind [ve-yirkesu] the breastpiece [et-ha-khoshen] by its rings [mi-tabb'otav] to [el] the rings [tabb'ot] of the ephod [ha-efod] with a cord [bi-fetil] of blue [tekhelet], to be [lihyot] upon [al] the band [kheshev] of the ephod [ha-efod]; and the breastpiece [ve-lo-yizzakh ha-khoshen] shall not come loose [me-al] from the ephod [ha-efod].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְיִרְכְּסוּ אֶת־הַחֹשֶׁן מִטַּבְּעֹתָיו אֶל־טַבְּעֹת הָאֵפוֹד בִּפְתִיל תְּכֵלֶת לִהְיוֹת עַל־חֵשֶׁב הָאֵפוֹד וְלֹא־יִזַּח הַחֹשֶׁן מֵעַל הָאֵפוֹד. **Key terms:** יִרְכְּסוּ (yirkesu) = qal imperfect of רכס (rakhas, "to bind, tie") — a rare verb. יִזַּח (yizzakh) = niphal imperfect of זחח (zakhakh, "to move away, slip") — "shall not slip away"; the breastpiece must remain firmly attached.

**29** And Aaron [ve-nasa Aharon] shall bear the names [et-shemot] of the sons of Israel [benei Yisrael] in the breastpiece [be-khoshen] of judgment [ha-mishpat] upon [al] his heart [libbo] when he goes in [be-vo'o] to the holy place [el-ha-qodesh] — as a remembrance [le-zikkaron] before [lifnei] YHWH continually [tamid].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְנָשָׂא אַהֲרֹן אֶת־שְׁמוֹת בְּנֵי־יִשְׂרָאֵל בְּחֹשֶׁן הַמִּשְׁפָּט עַל־לִבּוֹ בְּבֹאוֹ אֶל־הַקֹּדֶשׁ לְזִכָּרֹן לִפְנֵי־יְהוָה תָּמִיד. **Key terms:** עַל־לִבּוֹ (al-libbo) = "upon his heart" — the names on the shoulders (v. 12) carry the burden; the names on the heart carry the intimacy. The priest bears Israel both in strength (shoulders) and in affection (heart) before YHWH. תָּמִיד (tamid) = "continually" — the remembrance is perpetual.

**30** And you shall put [ve-natattah] into [el] the breastpiece [khoshen] of judgment [ha-mishpat] the Urim [et-ha-Urim] and the Thummim [ve-et-ha-Tummim]; and they shall be [ve-hayu] upon [al] the heart [lev] of Aaron [Aharon] when he goes in [be-vo'o] before [lifnei] YHWH; and Aaron [ve-nasa Aharon] shall bear the judgment [et-mishpat] of the sons of Israel [benei Yisrael] upon [al] his heart [libbo] before [lifnei] YHWH continually [tamid].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְנָתַתָּ אֶל־חֹשֶׁן הַמִּשְׁפָּט אֶת־הָאוּרִים וְאֶת־הַתֻּמִּים וְהָיוּ עַל־לֵב אַהֲרֹן בְּבֹאוֹ לִפְנֵי יְהוָה וְנָשָׂא אַהֲרֹן אֶת־מִשְׁפַּט בְּנֵי־יִשְׂרָאֵל עַל־לִבּוֹ לִפְנֵי יְהוָה תָּמִיד. **Key terms:** אוּרִים (Urim) = "lights" — from אור (or, "light"); the plural of "light." תֻּמִּים (Tummim) = "perfections, completions" — from תמם (tamam, "to be complete, perfect"). The Urim and Thummim are objects placed within the folded breastpiece-pouch for determining YHWH's will (cf. Num 27:21; 1 Sam 28:6). Their exact nature is never described — the text assumes knowledge. מִשְׁפָּט (mishpat) = "judgment" — Aaron bears the judicial decision-making capacity for Israel on his heart. LXX: δήλωσιν καὶ ἀλήθειαν ("manifestation and truth") for Urim and Thummim — an interpretive rendering.

---

## The Robe of the Ephod (vv. 31–35)

**31** And you shall make [ve-asita] the robe [et-me'il] of the ephod [ha-efod] entirely [kelil] of blue [tekhelet].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ אֶת־מְעִיל הָאֵפוֹד כְּלִיל תְּכֵלֶת. **Key terms:** כְּלִיל (kelil) = "entirely, wholly" — from כלל (kalal, "to complete"); the robe is made entirely of blue (tekhelet), unlike the multicolored ephod and breastpiece. The robe is worn beneath the ephod.

**32** And the opening [u-fhi] of its head [rosho] shall be [yihyeh] in the midst of it [be-tokho]; a border [safah] shall be [yihyeh] for its opening [li-fhiv] all around [saviv], the work [ma'aseh] of a weaver [oreg] — like the opening [ke-fi] of a coat of mail [takhara] it shall be [yihyeh-lo]; it shall not be torn [lo yiqqarea].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהָיָה פִי־רֹאשׁוֹ בְּתוֹכוֹ שָׂפָה יִהְיֶה לְפִיו סָבִיב מַעֲשֵׂה אֹרֵג כְּפִי תַחְרָא יִהְיֶה־לּוֹ לֹא יִקָּרֵעַ. **Key terms:** פִי (fi) = "mouth, opening" — the head-hole of the robe. תַחְרָא (takhara) = "coat of mail, leather armor" — a rare word; the reinforced neckline is compared to armor. לֹא יִקָּרֵעַ (lo yiqqarea) = "it shall not be torn" — niphal imperfect of קרע (qara, "to tear"); a woven reinforcement prevents tearing.

**33** And you shall make [ve-asita] upon [al] its hem [shulaiv] pomegranates [rimmonei] of blue [tekhelet] and of purple [ve-argaman] and of scarlet yarn [ve-tola'at shani] upon [al] its hem [shulaiv] all around [saviv], and bells [u-fa'amonei] of gold [zahav] in the midst of them [be-tokham] all around [saviv].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ עַל־שׁוּלָיו רִמֹּנֵי תְּכֵלֶת וְאַרְגָּמָן וְתוֹלַעַת שָׁנִי עַל־שׁוּלָיו סָבִיב וּפַעֲמֹנֵי זָהָב בְּתוֹכָם סָבִיב. **Key terms:** שׁוּלָיו (shulaiv) = "its hems, its skirts" — from שׁול (shul, "hem, train"). רִמֹּנִים (rimmonim) = "pomegranates" — woven fabric pomegranates in the three colors (without gold). פַּעֲמֹנִים (pa'amonim) = "bells" — from פעם (pa'am, "to strike, beat"); small golden bells interspersed among the pomegranates.

**34** A bell [pa'amon] of gold [zahav] and a pomegranate [ve-rimmon], a bell [pa'amon] of gold [zahav] and a pomegranate [ve-rimmon], upon [al] the hem [shulei] of the robe [ha-me'il] all around [saviv].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads פַּעֲמֹן זָהָב וְרִמּוֹן פַּעֲמֹן זָהָב וְרִמּוֹן עַל־שׁוּלֵי הַמְּעִיל סָבִיב. Alternating pattern: bell — pomegranate — bell — pomegranate, around the entire hem.

**35** And it shall be [ve-hayah] upon [al] Aaron [Aharon] to minister [le-sharet]; and its sound [ve-nishma qolo] shall be heard when he goes in [be-vo'o] to [el] the holy place [ha-qodesh] before [lifnei] YHWH, and when he goes out [u-ve-tseto]; and he shall not die [ve-lo yamut].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהָיָה עַל־אַהֲרֹן לְשָׁרֵת וְנִשְׁמַע קוֹלוֹ בְּבֹאוֹ אֶל־הַקֹּדֶשׁ לִפְנֵי יְהוָה וּבְצֵאתוֹ וְלֹא יָמוּת. **Key terms:** לְשָׁרֵת (le-sharet) = piel infinitive construct of שׁרת (sharat, "to minister, serve") — priestly service. וְנִשְׁמַע קוֹלוֹ (ve-nishma qolo) = "and its sound shall be heard" — niphal perfect of שׁמע with vav-consecutive; the bells announce the priest's movements. וְלֹא יָמוּת (ve-lo yamut) = "and he shall not die" — the sound functions as a protective mechanism; unauthorized or unannounced entry into the holy place brings death.

---

## The Gold Plate — "Holy to YHWH" (vv. 36–38)

**36** And you shall make [ve-asita] a plate [tsits] of pure [tahor] gold [zahav], and you shall engrave [u-fattakhta] upon it [alav] engravings [pittuhei] of a seal [khotam]: "Holy [qodesh] to YHWH [la-YHWH]."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ צִּיץ זָהָב טָהוֹר וּפִתַּחְתָּ עָלָיו פִּתּוּחֵי חֹתָם קֹדֶשׁ לַיהוָה. **Key terms:** צִיץ (tsits) = "plate, flower, blossom" — from צוץ (tsuts, "to blossom, shine"); the priestly frontlet; the word evokes both shining metal and a blossoming flower. קֹדֶשׁ לַיהוָה (qodesh la-YHWH) = "Holy to YHWH" — the inscription on the high priest's forehead declares his consecration to the divine name. LXX: πέταλον χρυσοῦν καθαρόν ("a petal/plate of pure gold") for tsits zahav tahor; ἁγίασμα κυρίου ("holiness of the Lord") for qodesh la-YHWH.

**37** And you shall put [ve-samta] it [oto] upon [al] a cord [petil] of blue [tekhelet], and it shall be [ve-hayah] upon [al] the turban [ha-mitsnefet]; upon [el-mul] the face [penei] of the turban [ha-mitsnefet] it shall be [yihyeh].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשַׂמְתָּ אֹתוֹ עַל־פְּתִיל תְּכֵלֶת וְהָיָה עַל־הַמִּצְנָפֶת אֶל־מוּל פְּנֵי הַמִּצְנֶפֶת יִהְיֶה. The gold plate is fastened to the turban by a blue cord, positioned on the front.

**38** And it shall be [ve-hayah] upon [al] the forehead [metsakh] of Aaron [Aharon], and Aaron [ve-nasa Aharon] shall bear [et-avon] the iniquity [ha-qodashim] of the holy things [asher] which [yaqdihu] the sons of Israel [benei Yisrael] shall consecrate [le-khol] — for all [mattenot] their holy gifts [qodsheihem]; and it shall be [ve-hayah] upon [al] his forehead [mitsekho] continually [tamid], for acceptance [le-ratson] for them [lahem] before [lifnei] YHWH.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהָיָה עַל־מֵצַח אַהֲרֹן וְנָשָׂא אַהֲרֹן אֶת־עֲוֺן הַקֳּדָשִׁים אֲשֶׁר יַקְדִּישׁוּ בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל לְכָל־מַתְּנֹת קָדְשֵׁיהֶם וְהָיָה עַל־מִצְחוֹ תָּמִיד לְרָצוֹן לָהֶם לִפְנֵי יְהוָה. **Key terms:** עֲוֺן (avon) = "iniquity, guilt" — from עוה (avah, "to bend, twist, distort"); the priest absorbs the ritual imperfections of Israel's sacred offerings. לְרָצוֹן (le-ratson) = "for acceptance, for favor" — from רצה (ratsah, "to be pleased, accept"); the gold plate secures YHWH's acceptance of the offerings despite their imperfections. The priest's forehead bears both YHWH's holiness (the inscription) and Israel's guilt (the bearing function) simultaneously.

---

## The Tunic, Turban, and Sash (v. 39)

**39** And you shall weave [ve-shibatsta] the tunic [ha-khetonet] of fine linen [shesh], and you shall make [ve-asita] a turban [mitsnefet] of fine linen [shesh], and a sash [ve-avnet] you shall make [ta'aseh], the work [ma'aseh] of an embroiderer [roqem].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשִׁבַּצְתָּ הַכְּתֹנֶת שֵׁשׁ וְעָשִׂיתָ מִצְנֶפֶת שֵׁשׁ וְאַבְנֵט תַּעֲשֶׂה מַעֲשֵׂה רֹקֵם. **Key terms:** וְשִׁבַּצְתָּ (ve-shibatsta) = piel perfect of שׁבץ (shavats, "to weave in checker pattern") — "you shall weave in checkered work"; the same root as tashbets (v. 4). The tunic and turban are plain white linen (shesh) — no gold, no colored thread. The sash is embroidered work (ma'aseh roqem), a lower grade than the skilled craftsman's work (ma'aseh khoshev) of the ephod and breastpiece.

---

## Garments for Aaron's Sons (vv. 40–41)

**40** And for the sons of [u-li-vnei] Aaron [Aharon] you shall make [ta'aseh] tunics [khuttonot], and you shall make [ve-asita] for them [lahem] sashes [avnetim], and headdresses [u-migba'ot] you shall make [ta'aseh] for them [lahem], for glory [le-khavod] and for beauty [u-le-tif'aret].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְלִבְנֵי אַהֲרֹן תַּעֲשֶׂה כֻתֳּנֹת וְעָשִׂיתָ לָהֶם אַבְנֵטִים וּמִגְבָּעוֹת תַּעֲשֶׂה לָהֶם לְכָבוֹד וּלְתִפְאָרֶת. **Key terms:** מִגְבָּעוֹת (migba'ot) = "headdresses, caps" — from גבע (gava, "to be high, rounded"); distinct from Aaron's mitsnefet ("turban"); the ordinary priests wear rounded caps. The same dual purpose — le-khavod u-le-tif'aret ("for glory and for beauty") — applies to all priestly garments.

**41** And you shall clothe [ve-hilbashta] them — Aaron [et-Aharon] your brother [akhikha] and his sons [ve-et-banav] with him [itto] — and you shall anoint [u-mashakhta] them [otam] and you shall fill [u-milleita] their hand [et-yadam] and you shall consecrate them [ve-qidashta otam]; and they shall be priests [ve-khihanu] to me [li].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהִלְבַּשְׁתָּ אֹתָם אֶת־אַהֲרֹן אָחִיךָ וְאֶת־בָּנָיו אִתּוֹ וּמָשַׁחְתָּ אֹתָם וּמִלֵּאתָ אֶת־יָדָם וְקִדַּשְׁתָּ אֹתָם וְכִהֲנוּ לִי. **Key terms:** וּמָשַׁחְתָּ (u-mashakhta) = qal perfect of משׁח (mashakh, "to anoint") — the root of mashiakh ("anointed one, messiah"). וּמִלֵּאתָ אֶת־יָדָם (u-milleita et-yadam) = "and you shall fill their hand" — piel of מלא (male, "to fill"); a technical idiom for priestly ordination/installation; literally "hand-filling," likely referring to placing the inaugural offering in the priest's hands. וְקִדַּשְׁתָּ (ve-qidashta) = piel of קדשׁ (qadash, "to be holy") — "you shall consecrate, set apart."

---

## The Linen Undergarments (vv. 42–43)

**42** And you shall make [ve-aseh] for them [lahem] undergarments [mikhnesei] of linen [vad] to cover [le-khassot] the flesh [besar] of nakedness [ervah]; from [min] the hips [motnayim] and unto [ve-ad] the thighs [yerekhaim] they shall be [yihyu].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַעֲשֵׂה לָהֶם מִכְנְסֵי־בָד לְכַסּוֹת בְּשַׂר עֶרְוָה מִמָּתְנַיִם וְעַד־יְרֵכַיִם יִהְיוּ. **Key terms:** מִכְנְסֵי (mikhnesei) = "undergarments, breeches" — from כנס (kanas, "to gather, collect"); garments that gather around the lower body. בַד (vad) = "linen" — specifically white linen; distinct from שׁשׁ (shesh, "fine linen") — bad is the standard priestly linen. עֶרְוָה (ervah) = "nakedness, exposure" — from ערה (arah, "to be naked, exposed"); priestly service requires the covering of nakedness, preventing the exposure that characterized pagan worship. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις αὐτοῖς περισκελῆ λινᾶ — περισκελής ("covering the legs") for mikhnasei.

**43** And they shall be [ve-hayu] upon [al] Aaron [Aharon] and upon [ve-al] his sons [banav] when they go in [be-vo'am] to [el] the tent [ohel] of meeting [mo'ed], or [o] when they draw near [be-gishtam] to [el] the altar [ha-mizbeakh] to minister [le-sharet] in the holy place [ba-qodesh]; and they shall not bear [ve-lo yis'u] iniquity [avon] and die [va-metu] — a statute [khuqqat] of perpetuity [olam] for him [lo] and for his seed [u-le-zar'o] after him [akharav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהָיוּ עַל־אַהֲרֹן וְעַל־בָּנָיו בְּבֹאָם אֶל־אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד אוֹ בְגִשְׁתָּם אֶל־הַמִּזְבֵּחַ לְשָׁרֵת בַּקֹּדֶשׁ וְלֹא־יִשְׂאוּ עָוֺן וָמֵתוּ חֻקַּת עוֹלָם לוֹ וּלְזַרְעוֹ אַחֲרָיו. **Key terms:** בְגִשְׁתָּם (be-gishtam) = qal infinitive construct of נגשׁ (nagash, "to draw near, approach") — "when they draw near." וְלֹא־יִשְׂאוּ עָוֺן וָמֵתוּ (ve-lo yis'u avon va-metu) = "and they shall not bear iniquity and die" — the undergarments are not optional; neglecting them incurs guilt and death. חֻקַּת עוֹלָם (khuqqat olam) = "a statute of perpetuity" — the same formula closing the chapter as closed 27:21. LXX: νόμιμον αἰώνιον ("eternal ordinance") for khuqqat olam; κύριος for YHWH.

---

## Translator's Afterword

Exodus 28 transitions from sacred space to sacred person. Having completed the tabernacle and its furnishings (chapters 25-27), the text now addresses who may serve within them. The chapter's organizing principle is vestiture — clothing as theology. The garments are described from outermost and most complex (ephod, breastpiece) to innermost and simplest (linen undergarments), peeling back layers of the priestly person.

The ephod (vv. 6-14) is the foundational outer garment, made of five materials including gold thread — the same materials as the parokhet (26:31). The onyx shoulder-stones bearing Israel's twelve tribal names establish the priest's representative function: he carries the nation physically as he enters YHWH's presence. The repeated insistence that elements are mimmenu ("from it") — the band integral to the ephod (v. 8), the chains connected to the settings (v. 14) — continues the pattern of organic unity seen in the cherubim and the lampstand.

The breastpiece of judgment (vv. 15-30) is the chapter's theological center. The twelve gemstones — each engraved with a tribal name "like the engravings of a seal" — make the breastpiece a living document of Israel's identity over the priest's heart. Where the shoulder-stones convey burden (nasa, "to bear"), the breastpiece conveys intimacy (al-libbo, "upon his heart"). The Urim and Thummim (v. 30) — "lights and perfections" — are introduced without explanation, placed within the folded breastpiece-pouch. They transform the khoshen from ornament to oracle: the "breastpiece of judgment" (khoshen mishpat) is literally the instrument through which YHWH's judicial decisions reach Israel.

The robe's bells and pomegranates (vv. 31-35) introduce sound into the sacred space. The bells announce the priest's movements, and the consequence of silence is death (ve-lo yamut, "and he shall not die" — implying that without bells, he would). The gold plate (vv. 36-38) with its inscription qodesh la-YHWH ("Holy to YHWH") is the chapter's most concentrated theological statement: the priest's forehead bears YHWH's holiness while simultaneously bearing Israel's avon ("iniquity") from their offerings, securing ratson ("acceptance, favor") before YHWH. The priest is thus a mediating figure — marked with divine holiness, laden with human guilt, securing divine favor.

The chapter closes with the linen undergarments (vv. 42-43), the most basic layer, required on pain of death. The movement from glory (le-khavod u-le-tif'aret, v. 2) to the covering of nakedness (le-khassot besar ervah, v. 42) frames the entire priestly wardrobe: outwardly, the priest manifests divine splendor; inwardly, he remains mortal flesh requiring covering.

Key MT-LXX divergences: (1) The LXX extensively reorders the breastpiece section, moving material and rearranging the sequence of attachment instructions. (2) The LXX renders Urim and Thummim as δήλωσις καὶ ἀλήθεια ("manifestation and truth"), an interpretive translation that reveals how the translators understood these objects — as instruments of divine revelation and truth. (3) The LXX renders tsits (v. 36) as πέταλον ("petal, leaf, thin plate"), emphasizing the metallic form rather than the botanical metaphor. (4) The LXX translates qodesh la-YHWH as ἁγίασμα κυρίου ("holiness of the Lord"), substituting κύριος for the Tetragrammaton. (5) Throughout the gemstone list (vv. 17-20), the LXX stone names differ significantly from later traditional identifications, reflecting the uncertainty of gemstone nomenclature across ancient languages. (6) The LXX adds additional material in the breastpiece section not present in the MT, expanding the instructions for the chains and attachment mechanisms.
